title: Automated Email to Supervisor?
excerpt: Hello! I have a board for users to request and automatically receive a PDF certificate of completion for a course using DocExport. The user adds their email and the PDF is sent to them. This has worked really well but I …

posts.author: Dr_Microbe (Diana Cervantes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! I have a board for users to request and automatically receive a PDF certificate of completion for a course using DocExport. The user adds their email and the PDF is sent to them. This has worked really well but I had a request to have this file also sent to the person’s supervisor automatically as well. So, they would not include the supervisors email, it would be based on the facility the user stated was their worksite. I have columns for the worksite name, as well as a column that includes the generated PDF. Is it possible to set up an automation in which if a person states they work at a specific site the generated PDF would be sent to a specific email address? Each supervisor is not a user of guest on the board.
Thank you for any advice you can provide!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You could have a board with sites listed as the name, then a column that has the supervisor’s email address for that site.

Then on your board where they request, instead of typing in name of work site, they’d choose the worksite from the above board. You could then have a mirror column to pull in the corresponding email address?

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, you can easily do that with Supermail, a native monday add-on.

monday.com

SuperMail - monday.com Marketplace

Emails: Design, Send and Track directly from monday.com

posts.author: Dr_Microbe (Diana Cervantes)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you Noam, I will look into that and let you know if it works! Diana

posts.author: Dr_Microbe (Diana Cervantes)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Desiree, I will try that in combined with the SuperMail app. I let you know if it works! Diana

posts.author: Dr_Microbe (Diana Cervantes)
posts.cookedHtml: Update: I used mirror columns and SuperMail and was able to get this done. Thank you both for your suggestions!! Diana
